The '''Vanguard''' are a trio of Xeric's former commanders encountered in the [[Chambers of Xeric]].

Each Vanguard is weak to a specific attack style, following the [[combat triangle]]:
*The ranged Vanguard is weak to [[Melee]] attacks and can be identified by the rocks gathered beneath its tentacles.
*The melee Vanguard is weak to [[Magic]] attacks and can be identified by the tentacles raised up in front of it.
*The mage Vanguard is weak to [[Ranged]] attacks and can be identified by the lack of tentacles.

[[File:Fighting the Vanguard.png|thumb|left|350px|A group of players battle against the Vanguard.]]
{{clear|left}}

==History==
The Vanguard, whose real names are unknown, were a trio of elite officers that served under Xeric during his reign. Each officer specialized in a specific combat style, and when the three were together, it was said that they were unstoppable. Their skill quickly caught Xeric's attention, and he appointed them as commanders of his army.

When disloyalty was suspected amongst the populace, the Vanguard would order their troops into the dissidents' homes at night. Being authorized to employ any tactic to make the suspects talk, the Vanguards soon developed a reputation for being cunning on the battlefield, ruthlessness in interrogation and loyalty to Xeric. 

Despite their abilities, when the people of Great Kourend rose against Xeric's tyranny, the Vanguards fled to save their own lives, which Xeric and an aide took note of. The Vanguard later discovered Xeric's camp at [[Mount Quidamortem]], apologizing to Xeric for their failure and promised to serve him again, with Xeric treated them with respect in return, which initially confused the aide, knowing of their cowardice. At some point, Xeric brought the trio down to the caves to grant them more power, lowering their guard. They realized the deceit too late as Xeric, fuming at their betrayal, melted their bodies down and reforged them into crustacean-like creatures, their souls completely destroyed. Xeric coldly scolded the trio and ordered them to stand guard in the caves until the end of time. The follower, who accompanied Xeric as a witness, was traumatized by the event and recorded it so others who came would learn of the fate of those who betrayed Xeric.

==Mechanics==
The three Vanguards originally spawn underground. When a player gets near one of the holes, all three will burst from the ground.

All Vanguards attack three times with each attack; however, the [[magic]] and [[ranged]] Vanguards use AoE attacks (one always hitting the main target while two are launched elsewhere). The [[melee]] Vanguard simply attacks its target three times for every attack it performs. Protection prayers will not nullify their damage, but they will reduce it by a significant amount.
[[File:Vanguard health difference near threshold.png|thumb|left|200px|The swirling mass around a Vanguard, indicating that the difference between the three's health is approaching the maximum threshold.]]
To harm the Vanguards, damage must be '''spread equally''' to all three Vanguards. If just one of the Vanguard is heavily damaged while the others have much higher health (a difference of 40% in groups of four or less, and 33.3% in groups of five or more), the Vanguards will immediately shuffle and restore their health to full. As a warning indicator, a swirling mass of energy will appear around the Vanguards, which brightens in colour the closer they are to a forced heal.

To stop this from occurring, the Vanguards' health must be within similar range of the other Vanguards by the time they shuffle positions. Vanguards will shuffle every 20 to 36 ticks on their own.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1437925375535984642|date=14 September 2021|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20210915112056/https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1437925375535984642|archivedate=15 September 2021|quote=[How long do the vanguards stay attacking until they go back down to rotate, excluding the effects of healing which makes them go down instantly?] 20 - 36 ticks, I think.}} The Vanguards cannot be attacked while in their shell, but any damage dealt just before they shuffle will still count. When shuffling, they will lose aggression over their current target. If the damage is fatal but hits after the Vanguard goes into its shell, it will survive the hit and restore 1 health.

As uncontrolled high damage is detrimental, weapons with low damage per hit such as [[toxic blowpipe]] are highly recommended. It may also be worthwhile to not activate damage boosting prayers and unequip gear that increases damage if there is a risk of dealing too much damage. Knowing the maximum hit for each weapon used is helpful.

Stat drains are not recommended for Vanguards, as they will restore all drained stats upon retreating into their shell. Instead, opt for damaging specials.

{{DropsLine|name=Vanguard judgement|quantity=1|rarity=Always|altrarity=Never|gemw=No|raritynotes=<ref name=book group="d">The book is dropped once all Vanguards are dead. No longer dropped after reading the book.</ref>}}

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 26 June 2024
|type = hotfix
|change = Fixed an issue where the Vanguards were not appearing consistently due to a recent engine change.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 29 November 2023
|update = Chambers of Xeric Changes
|poll = Chambers of Xeric Room Improvements
|change = 
* Vanguards drops have been standardized with overload drop mechanics remaining unchanged.
* Melee Vanguard now drops 2 [[Xeric's aid]] and an [[elder potion]].
* Ranged Vanguard now drops 1 Xeric's aid, 2 [[revitalisation potion]]s and a [[twisted potion]].
* Magic Vanguard now drops 1 Xeric's aid, 1 [[prayer enhance]] and a [[kodai potion]].
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 28 May 2020
|update = Chambers of Xeric Improvements
|poll = Old School Content Poll 71: Gauntlet, Grotesques, CoX and more
|change = 
*Vanguards now have a visual cue that indicates whether their health is too high or low.
*The Vanguards will now heal themselves when their health differs by 40% in groups of four or less rather than 33%.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 21 May 2020
|update = Vanguard Improvements
|poll = Old School Content Poll 71: Gauntlet, Grotesques, CoX and more
|change = 
*Vanguards' defence levels were lowered and their hitpoints were increased.
*The melee Vanguard's magic and melee defences were swapped to better suit the [[combat triangle]].
*The Vanguards' aggression range was reduced and one of the spawn positions was shifted.
*At least one overload drop is guaranteed from a Vanguard room.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}
